scores.correctness_notes: The response correctly shows the IngressPerAppRequirer class, the requires-side relation declaration, key events (ready/revoked), and the URL property. The gold standard notes v1 as the example but v2 also exists and is valid. The implementation pattern aligns well with what the gold standard implies.
scores.hallucination_notes: Most APIs appear genuine (IngressPerAppRequirer, on.ready, on.revoked, url property, strip_prefix, redirect_https). Minor uncertainty around `is_ready()` (may be named differently in practice) and `provide_ingress_requirements()` as a runtime alternative — these are plausible but not fully verifiable against the gold standard.
